title: About
text:
I’m Chris Lake, a data scientist specializing in causal inference and experimentation. I architect and lead rigorous experiments on millions of users, taking end-to-end ownership of the experimental lifecycle, from proposing synthetic control frameworks for flagship product revamps to engineering automated workflows that safeguard millions in annual spend.
At Booking.com, one of the world’s most sophisticated high-frequency experimentation environments, I lead experimental design for flagship products and bridge the gap between complex data infrastructure and high-impact business decisions.
I’m looking for a challenge where I can lead impactful deep-dives that define a company’s technical roadmap.

title: Projects
text: A collection of data science projects focused on causal inference, experimentation, and applied machine learning.

title: Projects
section: NBA 65-Game Rule — Causal Analysis of Injury Rates
text:
NBA 65-Game Rule — Causal Analysis of Injury Rates
Methods: Difference-in-Differences Domain: Sports Analytics Status: In progress
The NBA introduced a 65-game minimum in the 2023–24 season to qualify for end-of-season awards. This project investigates whether the rule change had the unintended consequence of increasing injury rates among star players, using a DiD design that compares star players to a control group of “sub-star” players before and after the rule’s implementation.

title: Projects
section: Netflix Market Entry & Genre Box Office
text:
Netflix Market Entry & Genre Box Office
Methods: Difference-in-Differences Domain: Entertainment / Media Economics Status: Data collection in progress
When Netflix entered a new country, did it differentially suppress certain genres at the theatrical box office? This project uses Netflix’s staggered international expansion as a natural experiment, combining IMDb genre data with European box office admissions from the Lumiere database.

More projects coming soon.

title: Chris Lake
text:
I’m a data scientist specializing in causal inference, experimentation, and applied machine learning. I focus on answering why questions — not just what — using methods like difference-in-differences, synthetic control, and regression discontinuity.
I currently work in data science at Booking.com, where I build data products and analytical frameworks at scale.
